subject: Anthropology
title: Design Features of Language
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Design Features of Language.

hook.text: Bees dance to show where flowers are. But they can't discuss yesterday's flowers. Only human language can.

buildup.text: Charles Hockett listed design features that distinguish human language from animal communication systems.

discovery.text: Key features include displacement (talking about absent things), productivity (creating new sentences), and duality.

twist.text: Some primates learn signs, but none combine them with full grammar. The gap between animal and human language persists.

climax.text: Hockett's features show that language isn't just more communication — it's a fundamentally different kind of system.

punchline.text: Human language doesn't just say more — it does more.

quiz.question: What is displacement in language?
options: Talking about things not present in the immediate environment
Replacing old words with new ones
